surface: Vite harness (apps/desktop/bench/web) mounting the real CommandPalette + SidebarNoteRow
engine: Google Chrome / Chromium via Playwright (browser MCP)
metric: React Profiler actualDuration (ms) summed over an interaction; wallMs includes requestAnimationFrame waits and is not the work cost
iterations: 3 measured runs after 1 warm-up; baseline via git checkout of the 5 source files at 7225f2e

palette-typing-10-keys-50-results.interpretation: Real Chromium React commit time ~24.5ms -> ~20.1ms (~18% lower) because memoized Snippets skip parseHighlights within each commit.

palette-arrowdown-15.note: Runs where ArrowDown reached the list end commit fewer times (~8) and are excluded from the ms comparison.
palette-arrowdown-15.interpretation: Stable key updates the preview in place (~29ms) vs path key remounting it each arrow (~36ms); ~20% lower commit time.

sidebar-rerender-12x40rows.interpretation: At 40 rows the per-commit cost is small; the real-browser wall-clock is within run-to-run noise, so the deterministic row-execution count (480 -> 0) is the reliable signal here.
